frozen_bugs._read_me: These cells are WRONG ON PURPOSE. Do not correct them -- they are the baseline campaign steps S2-S6 are measured against. Full rationale: integration-tests/requirements_corpus/frozen_bugs.py

FV-1.flipped_by: S4 (one header-driven row parser -- column order stops being load-bearing, so a zero-row parse of a populated spec becomes impossible)
FV-1.what: A spec that parses to ZERO rows makes traceability check T1 SKIP rather than FAIL.

FV-2.flipped_by: S6 (one catalog at one path: 'no requirements found' stops being a legitimate state)
FV-2.what: An empty requirement set reads GREEN across the audit plane -- 'no requirements' is treated as 'nothing to audit'.

FV-3.flipped_by: S4 (a header-driven reader selects by column NAME, so a row wider than its header can no longer shift the body)
FV-3.what: The RTM can display the WRONG requirement text.

FV-4.flipped_by: S4 (one header-driven reader, one accepted id column)
FV-4.what: Group I silently audits NOTHING when a table header says 'FR' instead of 'ID'.

FV-5.what: Group I drops requirement rows that appear after any heading.

regenerated_for: S3: Requirement.namespace became a read-only property derived from the FR id (manifest v3), so the corpus serializer -- which walks dataclasses.fields() -- no longer surfaces it. 38 cells lose their "namespace": "<directory-name>" entry in the single target parse._requirement_parse.parse_requirements. Zero additions; no spec_path or split cell moved, so every id-only target is untouched.
